claim: Multi-agent systems excel when problems are open-ended and difficult to predict beforehand; use workflows for well-defined, hard-codeable steps instead
actionable: Use single-agent workflows for deterministic tasks with clear step sequences; reserve multi-agent systems for problems requiring dynamic decision-making and adaptive task decomposition
source_title: How to Build Multi AI Agents with Langgraph

claim: Use a more powerful model for the supervisor in multi-agent systems while cheaper models can handle specialized subtasks
actionable: Deploy a stronger LLM (e.g., Claude 3.5 Opus) as supervisor for orchestration and decision-making, with cheaper models (e.g., Claude Haiku) handling focused agent tasks
source_title: How to Build Multi AI Agents with Langgraph

claim: Separating context between agents improves focus and reliability; cramming all tools and prompts into one agent creates confusion
actionable: Dedicate each agent to a specific domain with its own system prompt, tools, and state; avoid monolithic agent architectures when handling multiple distinct task categories
source_title: How to Build Multi AI Agents with Langgraph

claim: Multi-agent systems achieve context window multiplication by giving each agent its own reasoning tokens, scaling performance nearly linearly with agent count
actionable: When reasoning tokens become a bottleneck in single-agent systems, add specialized agents to distribute the computational load across multiple context windows
source_title: How to Build Multi AI Agents with Langgraph

claim: Use Langgraph's command primitive (with update, go_to, graph args) to route between agent subgraphs and update shared state
actionable: Leverage command objects in tool definitions to specify state updates and next node routing, bypassing default tool-to-agent flow for complex orchestration
source_title: How to Build Multi AI Agents with Langgraph

claim: Parallel tool calling in supervisors can break sequential workflows; disable it when agents depend on prior agent outputs
actionable: Set parallel_tool_calls=False in supervisor LLM binding when agent execution order matters (e.g., researcher before copywriter)
source_title: How to Build Multi AI Agents with Langgraph

claim: Breaking research into atomic subtasks helps supervisors course-correct; multi-call handoffs produce better outputs than monolithic requests
actionable: In supervisor prompts, encourage agents to decompose complex requests into multiple focused subtasks and iterate on intermediate outputs
source_title: How to Build Multi AI Agents with Langgraph

claim: Using Langgraph subgraphs instead of tool-based agent calls provides better observability and tracing of individual agent execution
actionable: Build multi-agent systems as connected subgraphs in Langgraph rather than calling agents via tool APIs to enable full execution visibility
source_title: How to Build Multi AI Agents with Langgraph

claim: Pass minimal context to agent subgraphs (single task message) while supervisor maintains full conversation history
actionable: When invoking subgraph agents, send only the specific task/message, not the full supervisor conversation history, to keep agent context focused
source_title: How to Build Multi AI Agents with Langgraph

claim: Modularized agents enable reuse across different applications; optimize agents independently without affecting others
actionable: Design agents as reusable modules with shareable prompts, tools, and config; use the same research agent across content creation, competitor analysis, and prospecting systems
source_title: How to Build Multi AI Agents with Langgraph
